prompt:
After regenerating the fleet API client, both the scheduler and the operator CLI started failing immediately on short control-plane busy responses. Logs now show `fleet API 503 fleet_busy`, even though the service supplies a retry delay and the older busy response is still retried successfully. Other generated problems, including not-found, continue to need the stable errors our callers already use.

Please orient through the generated API package, the handwritten compatibility client, and both consumers. Keep generated/client.gen.go untouched; it is regenerated from the service contract. Repair retry and translation at the handwritten seam so every current consumer gets the behavior once. Retries are bounded by the configured attempt count, use the server delay when present, preserve the legacy busy behavior, and retain generated error causality after stable translation. Context cancellation and unrelated API problems must not be retried or swallowed.

Run `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...`. The test, go.mod, and generated client are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: locate the handwritten compatibility adapter around generated Go API code and update retry and error translation there for all consumers
full_distill.baseline_evidence: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... fails because a generated 503 fleet_busy Problem reaches scheduler and CLI after one call instead of retrying with RetryAfterMillis
full_distill.success_contract: legacy and typed fleet-busy responses retry within the configured bound through injected delays, successful results reach scheduler and CLI, and exhausted/not-found errors retain stable classification plus generated cause identity without changing generated code
adversarial_factors: scheduler, CLI, and direct adapter tests all exercise the typed response, so consumer-specific retry blocks leave other callers broken
the generated file is protected and compile-time interface assertions use its exact types, rejecting edits to regenerated API code
legacy sentinel retries, typed retry delays, bounded exhaustion, and errors.As/errors.Is causality are asserted together, so replacing compatibility or swallowing the last error fails
a different 503 code and context cancellation must return after one call with no injected delay, rejecting status-only retries and broad transient handling
